OPPOSITION TO MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 AND 50.1; REQ. FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLTF AND PLTF'S COUNSEL; DEC. OF DOUGLAS B VANDERPOOL FILED BY DEFENDANT WILLGEROTH, CARL F. WILLGEROTH, BEVERLY R.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCES, THE PARTIES ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO A JUDGE PRO TEM RULING ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 IS DENIED. THE VERIFIED STATEMENTS OF PAUL AND BEVERLY WILGEROTH IN THEIR SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES EXPLAINING THE FACTUAL BASIS FOR DEFENDANTS' GENERAL DENIAL SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 ARE ALSO NOT REQUIRED TO IDENTIFY THE PARTICULAR ELEMENT OF EACH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE SUPPORTED BY ANY PARTICULAR FACT. BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PROTEM: ERNST HALPERIN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CARL WILLGEROTH AND BEVERLY WILLGEROTH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT CANNOT NOW SAY THAT THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CLAIM AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF IS ADMONISHED THAT THIS MAY BE HIS LAST OPPORTUNITY TO TRY TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CLAIM AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS. PARTIES SUBMITTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES REGARDING (1) THE CONTINUED VALIDITY OF THE FIRST PROMISSORY NOTE, (2) THE EFFECT, IF ANY, OF THE SECOND PROMISSORY NOTE, AND (3) WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS SUPPOSED TO SUBMIT THE UCC-1 FINANCIAL STATEMENT TO ESCROW IN ORDER TO BE PAID ON THE FIRST PROMISSORY NOTE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Douglas B. Vanderpool, Esq., Law Offices, 714-828-8946, defendant and opposing party, appearing by telephone

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied in its entirety. There are triable issues regarding (1) the continued validity of the first promissory note, (2) the effect, if any, of the second promissory note, and (3) whether Plaintiff was supposed to submit the UCC-1 financial statement to escrow in order to be paid on the first promissory note. Prevailing party to submit a form of order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org
Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Answer To The Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DOUGLAS B. VANDERPOOL, TEL. NO. (714)828-8946 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Answer To The Third Amended Complaint IS Denied. The motion serves no useful purpose. Plaintiff's remedy is to pursue the discovery motion to obtain the facts, if any, that support each of the purported defenses. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES.
There being no appearances, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to a Judge Pro Tem ruling on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 is denied. The verified statements of Paul and Beverly Wilgeroth in their Supplemental Responses to Form Interrogatories explaining the factual basis for Defendants' general denial substantially complies with the requirements of Form Interrogatory 15.1 Defendants' responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 are also not required to identify the particular element of each affirmative defense supported by any particular fact. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed. (302/JPT) Judge ProTem: Ernst Halperin; Not reported

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CARL WILLGEROTH AND BEVERLY WILLGEROTH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained with ten days leave to amend. The court cannot now say that there is no reasonable possibility that plaintiff can state a claim against these defendants. However, plaintiff is admonished that this may be his last opportunity to try to allege a viable claim against these defendants. Parties submitted. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-02-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. Everett A. Hewlett, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Denise A. Leadbetter, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Prabhaben Patel, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Carl F. Willgeroth or Beverly R. Willgeroth.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, December 2, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Prabhaben Patel, and against the Defaulted Defendants Carl F. Willgeroth and Beverly Willgeroth as follows:

Damages/Principal - $265,170.40.
Prejudgment Interest - $34,752.15.
Attorney Fees - $8,471.75.
Late Fees - 4,375.05 for a total judgment in the amount of $312,769.35.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
